objectives: Refresh core concepts in vectors, matrices, derivatives, and probability needed for ML
Explain the basic supervised learning setup and common problem types
Connect mathematical intuition to simple learning algorithms
learning_path.title: Foundations: Math and Machine Learning Fundamentals

objectives: Write clean Python code for data handling and experimentation
Implement simple ML workflows end-to-end in Python
Structure code to support reproducible experiments
learning_path.title: Python for ML Workflows

objectives: Prepare raw datasets for modeling through cleaning and transformation
Design and validate features aligned with modeling goals
Prevent common data leakage pitfalls during preprocessing
learning_path.title: Data Preprocessing and Feature Engineering

objectives: Interpret model parameters and uncertainty using statistical reasoning
Apply inference concepts to compare models and draw conclusions
Relate statistical modeling assumptions to practical ML outcomes
learning_path.title: Statistical Modeling and Inference for ML

objectives: Explain objective functions and optimization goals in ML
Compare optimization behaviors and failure modes in practice
Connect optimization choices to training stability and performance
learning_path.title: Optimization Methods in Learning

objectives: Describe neural network components and how they learn representations
Identify common deep learning training issues and how to diagnose them
Relate architecture and optimization choices to generalization
learning_path.title: Deep Learning Concepts and Training Dynamics

objectives: Build and train neural networks using Pytorch
Implement training loops and track learning behavior
Organize experiments for repeatable model development
learning_path.title: Implementing Deep Learning with Pytorch

objectives: Design evaluation protocols aligned with modeling objectives
Compare models fairly using consistent benchmarking practices
Interpret results and identify sources of variance and bias
learning_path.title: Model Evaluation and Benchmarking

objectives: Explain attention mechanisms and transformer building blocks
Connect transformer design choices to performance and scaling behavior
Plan experiments to evaluate transformer variants
learning_path.title: Transformers and Attention Models

objectives: Formulate research questions and hypotheses for ML studies
Design controlled experiments and ablation studies
Define success criteria and analysis plans before running experiments
learning_path.title: Research Methods and Experimental Design for ML

objectives: Organize datasets, splits, and experiment artifacts for traceability
Define documentation practices that support reproducibility
Establish conventions for storing and retrieving research outputs
learning_path.title: Managing Research Data for Reproducible ML

objectives: Draft a coherent research proposal with clear aims and methodology
Align proposed experiments with evaluation and benchmarking plans
Practice professional communication in research and professional environments
skills: interact professionally in research and professional environments
learning_path.title: Writing Research Proposals and Professional Research Communication

matched_occupation.description: Computer scientists conduct research in computer and information science, directed toward greater knowledge and understanding of fundamental aspects of ICT phenomena. They write research reports and proposals. Computer scientists also invent and design new approaches to computing technology, find innovative uses for existing technology and studies and solve complex problems in computing.
matched_occupation.label: computer scientist
